CREATE TABLE test_schema.users (id INT, name VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE test_schema.orders (order_id INT, user_id INT, amount DECIMAL(10, 2));
INSERT INTO test_schema.orders (order_id, user_id, amount) VALUES (101, 1, 29.99);
INSERT INTO test_schema.orders (order_id, user_id, amount) VALUES (102, 2, 49.99);
SELECT u.name, o.amount FROM test_schema.users u JOIN test_schema.orders o ON u.id = o.user_id WHERE CURRENT_SCHEMA = 'test_schema';
CREATE TABLE another_schema.products (product_id INT, product_name VARCHAR(100));
SELECT (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_schema.users) AS user_count, CURRENT_SCHEMA;
